pattern: Mirror the tree by swapping each node's two children and recursing into both — a single post- or pre-order pass.
hints: Inverting a tree just means every node trades its left and right subtrees; the values never change.
If you can invert each child subtree, then swapping the two inverted children at the current node finishes the job.
Recurse to invert left and right, swap the node's left and right pointers, and return the node; the empty tree inverts to itself.

edge_inputs.description: perfect tree mirrors left to right
lopsided tree with holes — children must swap even when one side is missing

stress.description: random tree with holes; double inversion must restore the original
stress.note: first element non-null so a root exists
